<!-- Generated by `make validate-wavelength`. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Real-data validation of `opera_utils.nisar.get_nisar_wavelength`

Validation evidence for the wavelength-reader addition to
[opera-utils][opera-utils] proposed in [isce-framework/dolphin#704][issue],
at opera-utils commit `720bca9cb425049424015a430da5cc0466394eea`.

[opera-utils]: https://github.com/opera-adt/opera-utils
[issue]: https://github.com/isce-framework/dolphin/issues/704

## Method

The granules in `granules.json` (one per distinct (mode, polarization) configuration of `NISAR_L2_GSLC_BETA_V1`; see that file for the selection rule) were probed remotely via HDF5 byte-range reads over HTTPS -- no product was downloaded in full. For each granule, `centerFrequency` and `listOfPolarizations` were first read directly with h5py (raw reference); then `get_nisar_wavelength(url, subdataset)` was called for each sub-band present (with `subdataset = .../frequency{X}/<first polarization>`) and required to equal `c / centerFrequency` exactly, and for each sub-band absent it was required to raise a `ValueError` naming the missing dataset and the URL. `GslcProduct.get_wavelength` was spot-checked on one dual-frequency and one frequencyB-only granule.

Reproduce with `make validate-wavelength` (see README.md).

## Results: `get_nisar_wavelength`

| config | frequency | subdataset pol | centerFrequency [MHz] | wavelength [m] | vs raw `c/f` |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| 0005_NADV | frequencyA | — | *absent* | — | contextual `ValueError` |
| 0005_NADV | frequencyB | VH | 1,221.5 | 0.245429765 | exact |
| 0005_NASV | frequencyA | — | *absent* | — | contextual `ValueError` |
| 0005_NASV | frequencyB | VV | 1,221.5 | 0.245429765 | exact |
| 0005_NAXX | frequencyA | — | *absent* | — | contextual `ValueError` |
| 0005_NAXX | frequencyB | HV | 1,221.5 | 0.245429765 | exact |
| 0505_SVSH | frequencyA | VV | 1,221.5 | 0.245429765 | exact |
| 0505_SVSH | frequencyB | HH | 1,236.5 | 0.242452453 | exact |
| 2005_DHDH | frequencyA | HH | 1,229.0 | 0.243932024 | exact |
| 2005_DHDH | frequencyB | HH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 2005_DVDV | frequencyA | VH | 1,229.0 | 0.243932024 | exact |
| 2005_DVDV | frequencyB | VH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 2005_QPDH | frequencyA | HH | 1,229.0 | 0.243932024 | exact |
| 2005_QPDH | frequencyB | HH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 2005_SHSH | frequencyA | HH | 1,229.0 | 0.243932024 | exact |
| 2005_SHSH | frequencyB | HH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 2005_XXXX | frequencyA | HV | 1,229.0 | 0.243932024 | exact |
| 2005_XXXX | frequencyB | HV | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 4005_DHDH | frequencyA | HH | 1,239.0 | 0.241963243 | exact |
| 4005_DHDH | frequencyB | HH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 4005_SHSH | frequencyA | HH | 1,239.0 | 0.241963243 | exact |
| 4005_SHSH | frequencyB | HH | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 4005_XXXX | frequencyA | HV | 1,239.0 | 0.241963243 | exact |
| 4005_XXXX | frequencyB | HV | 1,293.5 | 0.231768425 | exact |
| 7700_SHNA | frequencyA | HH | 1,257.5 | 0.238403545 | exact |
| 7700_SHNA | frequencyB | — | *absent* | — | contextual `ValueError` |

## Results: `GslcProduct.get_wavelength`

| config | frequency arg | outcome |
|---|---|---|
| 2005_QPDH | A | `0.243932024` m, matches raw `c/f` |
| 2005_QPDH | B | `0.231768425` m, matches raw `c/f` |
| 0005_NASV | B | `0.245429765` m, matches raw `c/f` |
| 0005_NASV | A | raised `ValueError` (expected: sub-band absent) |

## Summary

- **22/22 present sub-bands**: `get_nisar_wavelength` returned exactly `c / centerFrequency` (bit-identical float), across all centre frequencies previously measured in `center_frequency.md`.
- **4/4 absent sub-bands**: the reader raised a `ValueError` naming the missing dataset path and the file URL instead of guessing.
- **4/4 `GslcProduct.get_wavelength` checks**, including the frequencyB-only granule where requesting the default `"A"` correctly raises -- supporting the design decision not to silently default to `frequencyA`.

## Environment

- opera-utils commit: `720bca9cb425049424015a430da5cc0466394eea` (git rev-parse of the local install source)
- python: `3.12.3`
- opera_utils: `0.25.8.post1.dev2+g720bca9cb`
- h5py: `3.16.0`
- Earthdata Login via `~/.netrc` (mode 0600); transport: `opera_utils._remote.open_h5` HTTPS byte-range reads

## Disclosure

These investigations were assisted with Claude Opus 4.8, Fable 5 (Anthropic), and GPT-5.6 Sol (OpenAI).
